reviews.review_text: incredible views!! very quite to have a relax holiday!! excellent facilities..hard recommended 🫶🏼

Loved our stay, relaxing and quiet.
Will be back for sure
Such an amazing location right on the beach without all the fancy caravan parks it's a genuine old school camp which allows fires.

Lovely small but busy camp ground. We had a site one row back but seemed almost on beach as we were near a walk way. We had water and power but had to hold grey water. They have a dump site. Some midgies at dusk. Amenities are large. The on site cafe Rusty Pelican is fantastic,  great fish and chips.
Great caravan park. Sandy! Right on the beach. Beautiful sunsets.

Schöner Campingplatz direkt am Wasser. Bar mit Live Musik und Blick auf den Sonnenuntergang. Perfekter Spot
Spielplatz und bbq direkt nebenan. Direkter Zugang zum Wasser, wir fanden es sehr schön hier. Stellplatz mit Strom

The beach front sites are great although some can be a little tight between the trees but that's the natural setting. You have to catch your grey water on those sites but most just tip it out there rather than walk to the dump point. It'd be nice to see grey water infrastructure in place. Also the fire pits are busted and broken. Surely a bit of upkeep shouldn't be too hard. The place is generally packed. Give the customer something back. I'll be back though.
Great place to stay awesome sunsets at the beach
Best place to stay in the beach. We have been a few times now and will go back. Great spot, awesome cafe onsite with live music and the best food. Gluten free options  available. A must visit!!

INCROYABLE !
Un camping superbement placé, au calme en bord de mer, très bien entretenu.
Les emplacements sont pas bien grands mais ils sont les pieds dans l'eau et c'est vraiment un rêve.
On peut y pêcher, faire des feux sur la plage, des barbecues, bref allez y au moins 2 ou 3 nuits pour en profiter ...

Protected camp ground suitable for fishermen, crabbers & families.|We were none of those groups.|There is little grass just sand/dirt.|Beautiful sunsets for happy hour on the beach.|Clean amenities & very basic camp kitchen, small microwave, BBQ & large fridge.|Rusty Pelican on site for food/coffee/drinks
What a peaceful and wonderful place to sit back and watch the world go by

Great park,right on the water,excellent views,the staff are very nice,I must give a shout out to the,groundsman,a jovial Irishman,by the name of Ricky,I'm a bit of a novice at this caravanning caper,which he spotted pretty well straight away,and from that moment. Onwards,he was very helpful to me regarding,what to do and what not to do,he even pointed out another great c/ park to visit in yepoon,,ingenia,at kinda beach,cheers good call on that ricky
A very rustic camping ground.  The marked spots are small but some are right on the beach front.  From my spot at 27 I was only about 5 metres to the water.  The bathrooms are clean and functional.

Nice park. Great location
Friendly staff. Great licenced Cafe with live music some days. Food is very delicious and a reasonable price. We stayed in a caravan at the park but other than laundry we didn't use toilet and shower facilities.  I did walk in and they were clean. Yard maintenance was very good. Recommend
Amazing … best spot for the Agnes Water Blues Festival ❣️
SSH, don't tell anyone how amazing this place is!

This caravan park is beautiful and perfectly located. During peak season it was quite busy, and we found it a little tricky to get into our caravan site due to the number of vehicles and the coconut trees creating a tighter turning space.

Camping really close to the beach with amazing sunsets. The campsites are quite small and sometimes not very flat. However, the amenities are good and everything is clean. If you want a beachfront site, you need to book well in advance. Really nice campsite!

⭐️

We recently stayed at this campsite, and while we appreciated the warm welcome at reception and stunning location, our experience was somewhat disappointing. We booked last minute due to a cancellation, but upon arrival, we were shocked by how cramped the site was.

Unfortunately, I wish I had read the reviews beforehand. The campsite was overcrowded, making it feel more like a car park than a camping spot. It was frustrating to see multiple cars per van taking up valuable space.

Not felt as un-welcome at a camp spot in our lives. We had plenty of funny looks when we arrived and were told by x4 separate holidayers where we should be parking the caravan. The atmosphere seemed quite clicky during our visit, and I’m uncertain if this is a common occurrence throughout the year. Ended in not many people interacting with us which was uncomfortable.

The toilets and showers were acceptable, but given the lack of space, it was challenging to enjoy the environment fully. As someone who runs a Hipcamp, I’ve taken away many tips on how not to run a campsite. It's such a shame because the location truly is beautiful!

We stayed here in Dec, and boy are we glad we did. From the minute we checked in, the staff were super friendly and helpful, happy to have a chat and share a laugh. The campsites are quite small (though we do have a fairly big setup), but they are right on the beach. Even the furthest sites are still less than 200m from the waters edge, and water views where ever you are. The amenities are clean and well maintained (even in busy times). Its a fishermans paradise (fishing, crabbing, prawning). Boat ramp close by and most people leave their boats and tinnies anchored near shore, out the front of the park. The sunsets are next level (especially for the East Coast). We loved it so much that we extended! We will be back! Thanks for a great stay!!

Perfect location. Facilities are clean and functional. Powered sites are available.
This is our second visit and are pre-booking for more.

What a total gem of a holiday park. Donna and Ricky are legends, really made for a great stay!
The cafe onsite is so great! Honestly you can just stay put and have the most relaxing holiday, we will definitely be back!
Thanks guys 🙌🏻🙌🏻

Great location and lovely stay for 2 nights , I booked 3 nights and asked for a refund for the last night as I decided to head south a day early as big drive home. The management refused to reimburse the pre paid 3rd nighteven though I left before 10am .Be careful when booking .
Because of this lack of service I wouldn't recommend booking and paying ahead A pretty spot but lacked personal service ..bummer ! The boat  trip to Lady Musgrave was fab 👌!

Haben hier 2 Nächte gestanden. Die Lage des Platzes ist gut, das war es aber auch schon. Insgesamt macht der Platz einen schmuddeligen Eindruck, die Sanitäranlagen waren ungepflegt, das Grauwasser musste in einen Eimer abgelassen und in einem Schacht am Sanitärhaus entsorgt werden. Eine Entsorgung des Inhalts der Chemie-Toilette war nicht möglich. Teilweise steht man dort sehr eng. Ich kann den Platz nicht weiterempfehlen.

Great powered camp grounds.
Right by the water.
Clean rest rooms.
Suitable for tents or caravans
Awesome place for the kids to hang out.
Awesome location, sites small.

Close to water, other than that a very dirty and packed camp site. Not enough grey water drains, so some people just let it go on the ground at night. Facilities were dirty and not maintained. Would not go near the kitchen area.
On the beach people great staff awesome views even better fishing everyone is like family
Right on the water ,great location
We stayed in a caravan sites decent size just tricky to get in and out of if you have vans across from you

Location fantastic, camp site very small.
Camp ground needs a good revamp, looking very tied.
Great campsite right beside the beach with lots of well kept facilities

While the park location is spot on , the park leaves a lot to be desired the park needs lots of attention in all areas.. sites are jam packed showers are ancient, amenities old as, have to continually empty grey water.. in this age grey water should have pipes throughout park.. while fishing tables are straight onto the ground for flies to breed.. not happy at all..

The beach is absolutely beautiful
The park it's self is dirty and over crowded and people shoved in and under maintained.
The grey water should all be piped on every site. For the cost of the stay they should have a dump point
Sorry I won't be back
Lovely place to camp. Right on the beach. Lots of shade trees and good amenities. Glorious location, watch the sun set over the water!

Kurz gesagt,  kann ich die gute Bewertung nicht nachvollziehen.  Dass der Campground am Wochenende randvoll ist, ist zu erwarten. Aber:
Wir zahlen für "powered", sollten aber den Wasseranschluss teilen.  Wie soll das gehen, wenn alle Anschlüsse belegt sind, keiner der Nachbarn da ist und man selbst wenig Zeit hat?
Beim Strom war unser Anschluss nicht in Reichweite und nur dank der Hilfe eines anderen Campers konnte mit Verlängerung angeschlossen werden.
Die Plätze hatten teils Furchen, viele hatten kaum Schatten und sind so dichtgedrängt, dass man oft  andere Plätze überqueren muss.
Der Strand war enttäuschend. Er ist relativ schmal und in der kleinen Bucht, nah am Ufer, liegen Dutzende von Booten und auch Jetski. Das mag für Bootsbesitzer gut sein, für uns nur lästig und störend.
Für die schönen Sonnenuntergänge muss man auch nicht auf diesen Campingplatz.  Am besten geht man vom naheliegenden,  sehr schönen Aussichtspunkt in Richtung Meer und sucht sich eine der vielen Stellen, die einen tollen Blick bieten.
Wir haben jetzt schon mehr als 20 Campgrounds besucht. Dieser hat ein schlechtes Preis-/Leistungsverhältnis.

Beautiful spot… however, the amenities are quite run-down.
They should probably reconsider their pricing, as it doesn’t reflect the actual quality of the campground.
Great location friendly staff
Great place to bring the family, Do recommend the garden deluxe cabin's.
We loved our stay at 1770 campground! Staff were really nice & helpful, site was big, amenities were clean & the location is perfect!! (Extra points for the restaurant at the front of the park 😍)

Fantastic spot on the beach, sunset drinks on the beach, nice bar and restaurant. Couple of problems were that even though we gave our van length when booking, the site was a metre short. Some sites had grey water drains but others didn't which was a pain needing to empty grey water buckets regularly. Should be offered a discount for the less service.
Great spot, "camping ground" very tight to get a caravan in!

The Vine Boys enjoyed our stay Fishing was not what it said in the Brochure😝😝😝lotsa whales But the location is wonderful,,And thanks Cedric for looking after our boat trailer and car while we fished the reef for the few nights we were there

Great location right next to the water. Lovely spot for sunsets. Campground is a bit packed with tight spots. Amenities older but clean and there are washing machines. They need to improve lighting at night through out the park especially for when visiting amenities after dark.

Everything is 5 star except the grounds which are 1 star at the best. No raking of sites so build up of debri is the norm, bbq pits never cleaned, cleaning paved walkways. Looks terrible. It let's the other aspects down badly.
No water on these sites, open fires allowed but no fire extinguishers or water hoses nearby.
The Team at 1770 Campground are great.  The Office personnel and the on-site Caretaker are very pleasant and very helpful.  This is a magic Campground.  Thank you Team at 1770 Campground.

Wowowow had a beach front pitch which was stunning. We stayed for one night which was not enough for this area. We walked all along the headland walking tracks and saw whales from the view points. Sunset and sunrise from these points were amazing. Highly recommend getting up for sunrise and running up the hill like we did - the only ones there! Jealous of everyone with their camp fires. Good camp kitchen! Clean toilets and showers. Great water pressure and heat! Only odd thing was the position of the power points. It didn’t really make sense that the one closest to us was for two pitches over. Our power cable didn’t reach and had to park super close to the next pitch to be able to connect it and park the van right up against the road. Would still recommend to anyone just maybe take a longer electric cable just incase.
You go there for the location and then you put up with other things I guess. Still worth to go I think, the shallow clam water with hills behind, quite special!

A lovely park which was maintained well.The bathrooms need an upgrade and a bigger/ better camp kitchen ie: an area that you can sit/ relax in with comfy chairs,more cooking options TV ,vending machine. The unpowered sites were lovely but if you rely on solar its not practical.Suggest that if booking unpowered sites (@ approx $50 per night)that a heads up would be nice ie: perhaps ask the question if solar would be practical.
Apart from that it was quiet and friendly and the sound of the waves from the beach was lovely.
Lovely park, but the booking staff need to be more on top of what sites are suitable for large vans. Site 10 is not suitable for a 9m caravan!
Busy campground in season.
Great place for a drink and a meal at sunset
Awesome place
So amazing

Like all the other reviews, an amazing location. We had the best beach site A20
Staff were friendly and efficient with questions and checking in.
Best camp ground I've been to
Older campground that’s pretty packed in but you can’t beat the campfire and location! Hard to find spots like these now!
Mixed feelings. Nice peaceful location & friendly staff.  Small sites on top of each other & very old facilities. Beach front sites are slightly larger with views and fire pits
Great caravan and camping park. On the beach. Good service and well maintained amenities.
Booked a site then told an hour before check we can’t have the site and then upon arrival the new site allocated was also allocated to someone else shambles of a camp ground

No soy ningún experto en campings ya que no he visitado muchos. Pero nuestra experiencia aquí por el momento resulta satisfactoria. Es verdad que las instalaciones son un poco antiguas y podrían actualizarse un poco.
Excellent place to go fishing and crabbing, but very busy on weekends. Facilities are a bit old but cleaned every day.

Super séjour mais j’ai trouvé le camping un peu cher, obligé de payer un supplément pour venir en voiture. Sinon les toilettes et douche était propre et une super vue sur la mer. Seule bémol c’est qu’il font le ménage à 9h alors qu’ils ont un temps libre de deux heures entre 10h et 12h entre les départs et les nouvelles arrivées, sinon rien à dire super séjour à refaire!
Great campground on the water.
A very laid-back place with a great beach view. My family and I come here 1 - 2 times a year. The food is nice and and there're Foodwork, IGA, restaurants nearby with reasonable prices and good food.
Cooler Platz, direkt am Strand

This very old caravan park is renowned for its great location on a fabulous stretch of beach that enjoys sensational sunsets. The facilities however are very tired and well below par when compared to others. Best suited to those that don’t mind rougher conditions and an absence of a pool or playground for kids, but are however seeking a special experience on and near the water. Upgrades to sites and facilities are well overdue.
Enjoyed our 5 nights at this caravan/camping park immensely! Although facilities are a little dated, they were clean. And the location is perfect!
Een mooie camping, prima faciliteiten. Mooie omgeving, leuk strand. Waard om meerdere dagen te verblijven.
Fantastic service always helpful

Super, schöner Campground, direkt am Wasser mit herrlichem Blick in die Bucht! Große schöne Stellplätze, viele Sanitäreinrichtungen, die etwas älter sind jedoch sehr sauber. Super freundliches Personal an der Rezeption. Und wenn man Hunger hat, gibt es auch einen guten Imbiss mit gemütlichen Plätzen und Blick auf das Wasser.

Man kann lange Strandspaziergang entlang der Bucht machen

Wenn man einen Stellplatz in der ersten Reihe erwischt, hat man permanent einen gigantischen Blick. Ansonsten muss man einfach nach vorne laufen und sieht in die wunderschöne Bucht.
No rooms just camping spots and ablution block. Fantastic spot for young families, backpackers, and retired travellers.  Walk to the beach. Beach views. Still waters.

Camping right on the sand at 1770 Camping Ground was a real highlight—such a beautiful location with calm water perfect for paddling, kayaking, and safe fun for the kids. Being so close to the beach is a big plus. That said, the park gets very busy and sites are quite tight. There isn’t much grass on the grounds, so it can get dusty, especially when it’s dry. Still, if you’re after a beachside experience and don’t mind a bit of hustle and bustle, this place is worth it!

**1770-令人惊叹的美景第三次吸引我回来！**
从平静水面上的金色日落到原始海滩和未受破坏的自然景观，这个沿海天堂的每个角落都感觉像是一颗隐藏的宝石。宁静的氛围、温柔的海浪和空气中的魔力让人无法远离。
1770——你的魅力令人难忘。我一次又一次地回来，每次访问都感觉像是第一次。
Very unique waterfront park that’s literally on the sand. Super friendly reception team. Ideal spot for an escape from reality. Enjoyed fish n chips for dinner at the Rusty Pelican 5⭐️
Looks after our function 10 out of 10 thankyou ladies

Travelled to 1770 in a motor home. We were given a good location to set up. Unfortunately we had rain during our stay. The restaurant was adequate for short stays. The menu is basic. Nice change thou to cooking yourself😀
Great place
Stunning sunset
Great location and sites

We accidently sound this camp ground and paid to stay for the night.  We got a lovely beach front site for our caravan.  Amenities are old but clean,  although the main amenities block was closed for repairs when we were there, but the other one was fine as it wasn't super crowded at this time of year in late February.  We have a 24ft caravan so it was a challenge to get into our site, but eventually managed.  We loved the cafe there for yummies and coffee. We ended up extended our stay for 2 nights.  Highly recommended!
Beautiful place

The new owners are really lovely and considerate. Such a beautiful place to stay, I have been here multiple times and will be more in the future. Beautiful sunsets, constant calm water to swim in, shady trees, clean sandy beach, great fishing… honestly nothing to complain about. 🌟
Great location.

Very disappointed we have booked a number of sites well in advance for a large family reunion to be advised our waterfront sites have been moved along the back all our sites have been separated, and not together like we had planned. When trying to ring they don’t answer the phone for hours at a time the service is disgraceful.
One of the best places to enjoy the beautiful scenary while enjoying the beach and the kids having a great time. Its very clean and kid friendly. Nice for scenic walks along the beach.

I was here during Christmas 2024, but it was hectically busy.
Each family was an inch away from other groups.
Don't go here during pick times or else you won't enjoy.
Great place for the family to holiday. It was very busy when we where there as it was xmas
Really nice spot! Beachfront, Lots of toilets and showers, also laundry and other amenities. We had a great time there with my husband!  ice cream from pelican, next door, was amazing haha
This place was beautiful, now it's a disaster
a truly magnificent spot, a bit tight but worth it, especially in the non peak periods. be careful for midges, I got mauled here!

Best camping spot in Agnes waters/1770. Right on the water and very spacious spots. Can watch the sunset over the water and perfect for swimming. Toilets and showers cleaned regularly and pleasant to use.

I had a wonderful stay at the 1770 Camping Ground. The site is clean, spacious, and well-maintained, with plenty of shaded areas and a peaceful atmosphere. The facilities are excellent, offering everything you need for a comfortable stay, including hot showers and BBQ spots. A highlight of my visit was the friendly and helpful office staff, especially the lovely girl at the reception. She went above and beyond to assist with local recommendations and made me feel truly welcome. I highly recommend 1770 Camping Ground for anyone looking for a relaxing and enjoyable camping experience!
No rooms, camping and vans
Great location didn't stay but looks amazing

Wow! What a location
Amenities are okay, sandy but okay
Gotta take your grey water with you which is annoying but worth it for this location

Lovely view, nice area for a visit.
Not a place for a tent.
Gravel area with no grass, power was so far from our spot, our extension lead wouldn't reach.

We booked and fully paid for a powered waterfront caravan site many months prior to our visit. We gave the dimensions of our caravan which is 8 meters in length. During the course of our booking we received several emails confirming our booking  giving us 3 different site numbers throughout the leadup to our visit. VERY disappointed to arrive and our caravan could not fit on the allocated waterfront site. Having no other waterfront  sites available for our 5 night stay we were allocated a site as far from the waterfront as possible. Apparently a staff member took it upon themselves to alter our allocated site 2 days before our visit, meaning  our van would never fit on the allocated site. Beyond disappointed as we were so looking  forward to a waterfront site, fire pit and access to the bay. Would we recommend this place for customer service? No way! I had to request and chase up a refund for  the difference between site fees and am still waiting for management to feedback to me how and why this happened. Be warned and prepared as there is a big difference in sites at this camp ground.

This charming campground is perfect for families and young adults, featuring well-maintained facilities and a stunning beach area. The local café gets quite lively in the evenings, often hosting live music. We arrived after hours, but the check-in process was quick and seamless.
Old Skool park👍. Restaraunt staff just ordinary and don't know what's happening in their own place .... re music (lack of communication)
Built in the 60's the amenities have never been upgraded. Staff uninterested. Pity because it's a great location.

Muy buena ubicación, lo ideal es que te toque un sitio frente a la playa con lugar para el fuego, pero no tiene cemento de ese lado. No tiene descarga de aguas grises. Las instalaciones están limpias pero no son tan modernas como en otros parks de la costa este. Tiene buena seguridad, la semana que estuvimos había muchísima gente, pero todo ordenado y silencioso.

Ziemlich teuer für das was geboten wird.
Zu wenige Toiletten/Duschen und sehr versifftes Campkitchen

Great place to relax
And the view is 6 ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐
Great food at an amazing location.
Visited the monument by captain Cook, walked down to beach. Would have loved more info on how, why & where captain Cook came.
We had to drive to the restaurant or tours etc,  and the cabin was tired and showing its age. The location was great in terms of aesthetics,  pool,  kids playground, short, pleasant walk to surf.

The camp grounds are in a fantastic location however unless you are lucky enough to be on the beach front sites then the remaining sites are very cramped & noisy.  After complaining about one noisy site in particular nothing much was done. My stay was not a peaceful one & I ended leaving one day early. The new management need to control noisy sites. I won't be back.

Beach is awesome, views are great, but I have Never been so squashed in, unfortunately had a load mouth close by also which added to the bad experience.
If your coming ask for row A if possible as you have a little space around you
Stumbled on one of the best sunset DJ sets in the car park.
Staff are awesome , very accomodating . First night beach side view , brilliant , very busy park, amenities very clean and looked after .
Awesome spot to stay at 1770 Camp ground. A little tight to move the van around but spectular weather and perfect sunsets
Have been going here for 20 years, great spot

Lovely little campsite right on the beach with lots of trees to give shade. Facilities are simple and a bit dated, but well maintained by the groundskeepers. A huge plus for us was that they have a baby bath section in their toilet/shower blocks -  VERY HELPFUL when travelling with an infant. The sunsets here are absolutely spectacular. If you’re lucky to get a spot along the beach, you’ll literally be a few steps from the water at high tide. You can check in from 12pm. Check out is 10am sharp.

Very concerned about the lack of tree care around the park. Council tries to absolve themselves from any responsibility for branches falling but doesn’t seem to proactively look after the trees.
We were located. Camp site directly under a large tree that had a small branch broken hanging above the site. Requested the removal of this before the wind could dislodge it - waited until the next day then had to ask again. Expressed concerns to the office about the trees and asked when they were last checked as there were several dead branches in the tree above our site. They didn’t know when they were last checked and advised they needed to report to Council any issues. Whilst Council try to say they will not pay any damages I would be arguing this unless any tree management plan was in place.
Beautiful park close to the beach, some sites are very tight and people had trouble getting vans in easily. Beautiful sunsets.
Very quiet park with a walking track to the beach. Beautiful spot
Absolutely stunning! We camped right on the front of the beach, view was amazing! Had to watch some of the trees backing in but definitely worth it when you have such an amazing place to camp!

Tienen la localización y se han relajado. Es como un car park sin césped o buena infraestructura para la época o el precio o el nivel de Australia. Lo cierto es que los sunset son amazing y solo merece la Peña si coje un beachfront site
Eigentlich hatte ich mir mehr davon erwartet, sehr enge Stellplätze, wenig Comfort, eher Backpackergemeinschaft mit schmutziger Campküche. Die Landschaft ist überragend.

The location is 5 stars, the caravan park is over crowded, dusty and a bit dated.
Still worth a stay for the views.

Fantastic location. Interesting setup that could use some love, though facilities were kept to an ok quality. Little bit more love from managers, and customers themselves ( very hectic joint with kids etc) it could be perfect. Would stay again, hope to see progress.
It's tops
Great place for a holiday

This camping ground is so peaceful by the water.  The camp site places can be a tight squeeze due to some palm trees & poles & our caravans are bigger now !! We have been coming for a few years now & we love it. Under new management we hope they enjoy & stay awhile same as us!  Good luck with your cafe renovations it should be a great addition.
Awesome place to stay

A fabulous location, we stayed on a beachfront site and WOW what a view!  We were sure packed in tight and amenities could do with some updating but the view is worth it. Also great to be able to have a campfire
Customer service was really great.
We would highly recommend.
A great place to stay in our Caravan.

We had booked an unpowered spot, so disappointing to turn up and find out we have to pay for power regardless as they have a new policy in place that I was not aware of (website clearly states different price for non powered vehicle). Crazy price, such a tiny cramped space on a dirt patch.

Terrible experience. Amenities dirty. Teenagers were aggressive and loud, swearing and yelling late into the night. Was harassed by said teenagers for four days when I made a complaint regarding their late night behaviour. This included them letting off the hand break to the camper and following me around the camp-site constantly yelling insults. Worst and scariest camp trip ever.

Aggressive and loud teenagers meant our stay was dreadful. Complained to the admin after listening to loud swearing late into the night for 3 days. Said teenagers then targeted us, stalking me wherever I went, yelling insults loudly at me if I stayed in the campsite. Tried to get some peace by leaving for the day only to come back to them having released the handbreak on the camper. Other campers also felt intimidated but were too afraid to make a complaint. Facilities mostly worked, broken washing machine took our money but didn't clean the clothes.  Showers became a mud pit most nights by about 7. Some toilets didn't flush.
Rude lady, wasn’t helpful. Hung up the phone on me. Booking lost.

Stay – Mid June 2024

You can’t beat this location. We had a powered and watered site with direct beach access, we could also have a fire. We spent 4 days here and wished we stayed longer.

The park toilets and showers are great (if you’re off power and water), suggest bringing a bucket as there is no grey water drain at your site – grey is disposed via a communal drain near the facilities block.

We did a daily walk (~4 km to the look out and back) featuring spectacular views.

We just spent 6 nights on one of the beach front sites. Most of the sites have trees to get around, this will test your backing ability. They do supply good clean drinking water. Most sites are level and not grass. The reason is the sites don't get a break from one camper to the next one as it very popular. The camp kitchen needs a bit of TLC and new BBQ's. Both the toilet blocks need to have a face lift, they are cleaned very day, but the friendly staff.
This is place that can give you some of the best memories, so you  have go a least once as there a lot of people that go back to this camp site very year and love it. The sunsets are brilliant as the campsite is  facing west looking over the water.
Beautiful place to stay on the water. Fantastic and easy walk along the water to the boat ramp and marina.
Was camping and it’s a great place to stay and friendly staff

I had a disappointing experience with Greg's customer service at this location. His lack of helpfulness and rudeness were off-putting. Unfortunately, I didn't even get the chance to stay here. Not providing information about available sites for people to view on a map before calling is very unhelpful.
I suggest a different career path for Greg, as customer service doesn't seem to be his forte.

The camp is in the most amazing location, facing a natural lagoon with a great sunset view. The lagoon is shallow, so you can swim there. However, my impression was that the site is poorly maintained. The kitchen area was ill-equipped. Toilettes and showers were not as clean as you could expect. There were no dryers machines only wash machines. The site is busy.

Had heard so much about 1770 camp ground so it was interesting to finally get there. I would describe it as an old school foreshore camping area on a nice protected waterway. Yes it is attractive and relaxing but to me didn’t quite match the high expectation. It is a popular getaway for groups, there were plenty there as we were there across a long weekend. The sort after beach facing spots were crammed but the whole place was crammed as it was busy. You took your life in your hands at night on the tracks with kids ripping around uncontrolled in the dark on eScooters. The amenities are antiquated and due for an extensive update but there was consistent hot water. I can see why it’s popular with fishing and returning family groups but as an interstate traveler having now been there I probably will try somewhere else different rather than repeat visit but I do recommend at least one visit.

Phoned 28/4/2024 looking for suitable size site for our trailer, was advised of a site and then given no time to access the map I had up on my phone, basically the guy on reception hung up. Decided not to stay there, customer service is exceptionally poor.
Stellplätze, an denen noch normal gegrillt wurde, daher hohe Rauchentwicklung, zog in den Campervan hinein
What a cracker of a spot, right on the water, beach view, price was so good. This place is a little paradise 🙌

Location is picture perfect with a fishing/water play paradise in front of the park and beautiful swimming places with or without gentle waves, about a 10-15 minute walk down the beach. Caravan Park is basic but has everything you need, except there is no sullage points and no RV dump point on site (it is down the road about 3kms). Only a 5 min walk to a quaint pub with a great view., 15 minute walk to the marina with a Cafe, Bar/Pizza and a Fish and Chip Shop with the most delicious fish we've ever had!
Well worth a stay, we have been back 3 times in 6 years.

It's pricey ($51 unpowered beach view), but the campsite has some stunning spots right beside the beach! We were also welcomed very friendly. There were no mozzies when we camped (beginning of March), which is a big plus. Though they could really improve the camp kitchen. There's only one stove, and kitchen utilities are old and rusty. Would be great if there's also WiFi.

We were recommended by a local we met whilst staying at the NRMA Agnes Water Holiday Park to stay here. We were told the campground was by the beach and we could watch the kids play whilst sat on our camp chairs. So on our return trip from Airlie Beach we decided to stay here.
At reception we were told a night would be AUD 78, I thought it was a bit pricey but decided to stay. After parking our motorhome, I found the cable couldn’t reach the power outlet. So had to move the motor which meant blocking the neighbours can. The grounds were dirt, the missus said the toilets were dirty and there was no wifi. At this point went to reception for a refund stating we were only here for a few minutes and didn’t use any facilities but was refused. The manageress said no refund policy. I said I will write a nice review but she didn’t care.
A few minutes later the missus tried and amazingly she managed to secure a refund less 25 dollars admin fee. She said I was rude. . At no point was I rude. Why should I be rude if i was asking for a refund. Doesn’t make any sense.
We went back to the NRMA. Much better customer service and value.
Pięknie zlokalizowany camping. Są toalety, prysznice, jest czysto. Ogólnie ok.

Unfortunately the sign says at the front no dogs permitted.  The beach front right at the front door permits dogs.  I get some people don’t do the right thing but a bond would be better than a flat no, especially for a high tourist area.

I booked another caravan park, not right on the waterfront but still dog friendly.  So many people travel with their pets, especially full time travellers.
Very busy. Right on the beach. Sunsets are amazing
Staff are quite unhelpful over the phone. Fully booked and unwilling to provide any suggestions for alternative options. Now reconsidering whether we should visit 1770 altogether.
Great place to know Agnes and 1770. Loved the vibe. Lovely people ✨
Great
Good camping 🏕

Very nice location right by the beach. The rest was very disappointing. There is no reception nor any Wifi. Amenities weren‘t that clean. Only cold washing machine available and the dryers where broken. The individual camping sites are only dirt.
Its good for people who love nature and don‘t care about the rest.

Physically walked into reception and booked with a guy in 2022 we asked if he could tell us what vacancies on the beach front they had .. he said we will be waiting a year as beach front are always booked and hard to get .. he told us Nov 2023 .. we booked 4 sites right then we didnt need to pay a deposit at that time .. he said we were lucky to get these sites .. So almost 12 months later we had no contact from so called to them .. only to be told that we dont have a booking .. 4 family's had booked time off work . Animals booked for kennels . We are so devastated that this happened to us . I have to say this is unacceptable and we are so annoyed with this establishment. Dont offer customers on your website to take bookings in 12 months advance because clearly they stuff it up and give it to others.

Really 2 and a 1/2 star accommodation but a 5 star location. Don't come with huge expectations about the camping grounds. They are very busy and cram you in but the location and view make it worth it. Ultimately it is your decision 😁

Booked this site 4 months in advance and we’re told to reconfirm a week out. When we rang the bloke I spoke to had no record of our booking. Luckily there was a free site and we manage to get it. Could have been much worse. However the campsites themselves were nice but slightly smaller than usual. Alittle outdated but Nice clean amenities. Location is absolutely amazing and there a couple of bars and coffee shops within a walking distance. Fishing is great.

Stayed in a swag under ute awning
Beautiful place, I love experiencing 1770 camping  ground. We stay every year on a beach side spot, an awesome place to be if you are travelling on Australias East Coast.
Such a beautiful relaxing beachfront campground, I felt like I was in an island 🏝️ we were lucky to get in and enjoy a beachfront site. Can’t wait to return!

Physically walked into reception and booked with a guy in 2022 we asked if he could tell us what vacancies on the beach front they had ..  he said we will be waiting a year as beach front are always booked and hard to get .. he told us Nov 2023 .. we booked 4 sites right then we didnt need to pay a deposit at that time .. he said we were lucky to get these sites .. So almost 12 months later we had no contact from so called to them .. only to be told that we dont have a booking .. 4 family's had booked time off work .  Animals booked for kennels .  We are so devastated that this happened to us . I have to say this is unacceptable and we are so annoyed with this establishment. Dont offer customers on your website to take bookings in 12 months advance because clearly they stuff it up and give it to others.

Had 4 sites booked (reserved) for over 12 months for this November as it’s always booked out. My parents rang the office today to confirm,  and they stated there was no such booking made and no sites for us. Very bad booking system for the 21st century. Very disappointed on the camp ground staff. Won’t be bothering with them ever again. I’m guessing someone paid in full over our booking and they override our booking.

We have always wanted to camp on the beach front but could never secure a booking. So we asked them when they're next availability was for the beach. They told us 11 months away. So we took it, he wrote our names down. Now a week out from our booking, we call to confirm and they've given it away to someone else. After 11 months of waiting, booking time off work etc. Very disappointed. We were told "sorry your name isnt here" and that was it. Website advertises beachfront bookings available for 2024 and can book 12 months in advance yet They couldn't seem to do it for us this year. So very disappointing.
Awesome experience with the family!
If its school holidays dont even bother they will shut you down straight away find somewhere else

The lady I spoke to when booking was very nice and understanding when I called back of an issue I was having after my wallet was stolen while visiting another town. But the man who answered the phone when I tried to sort out my booking was extremely rude and swore at me. I am extremely disappointed and shocked someone in customer service/ accommodation industry would do such a thing.  I love the camp grounds and have visited many times with my children as it is a great family destination.
Great location but very crammed in with campers .

Come on 1770, Agnes Waters council, spend some money on your park, a fabulous location let down by poor maintenance. Holes in the road, dirty run down amenities, no sullage to sites, not enough taps to go round. This camp ground could be the best in Aust, needs some love.
Great location. Amenities are disgusting!

Fantastic location but very ordinary park.  All sites are dirt.    They could put synthetic grass mats like alot of other caravan parks, especially since they are charging the same price.  Amenities old and not very clean.... am fairly certain they were not cleaned in the 2 nights we were there.
Beach front sites have excellent views. Tight campsites. Very popular. Clean amenities.
Fantastic camping ground, we will be back..

Had booked 4 nights and left after 1.....got hemmed in by 3 families numerous kids bongo playing drums behind us and then a huge van 1mt beside us with parents + 3 older very loud kids. No consideration or respect given AT ALL. Asked management if they could move us to another empty site, but they don't shuffle. They were VERY apologetic and said they had noticed a huge difference in rudeness from families since Covid. Would NEVER GO BACK. Sites just way too SMALL for amount of campers.
Tight space for caravans but beautiful place
Absolutely stunning place. Right on the beach. Spacious camp sites. Lovely park walk around the cliffs to a look out.
Relaxing and, the sunsets ohhhhhhhh.
Great spot to camp as you are right on the beach. Clean amenities and lovely service. Definitely coming back.
I stayed @ the caravan park Agnes Waters  and it was very very good on my time there.
Good park right on the water , great sunsets, can have fires

Contrary to some of the negative reviews this camp ground is a most amazing site at the beach. Clean, yes old but perfect. It is a clean beach lots of lovely walks and many people are return campers. Fishing hiking. View of sunset over the ocean which is unusual for east coast Australia.

Great campgrounds, right on the beach. The sunsets here are amazing!! ❤️ You're allowed a fire if you have a self contained fire pit (no ash on ground) & can buy firewood ($20 cash) that gets delivered to your site (which lasted us all 3 nights). Lots of bush turkeys around so you can't leave food or rubbish out. Plenty of toilets (2 amenity blocks) with hot showers & laundry facilities.
It's such a beautiful spot to camp right on the beach. One of the cleanest facilities we have used while camping. No cabins, just camp sites. Owners were lovely & friendly.
Beautiful caravan park right on the beach! Very chilled out and peaceful. Friendly staff and excellent caretakers who have been there 6 months. We will definitely be back.

Zero stars, this park is run by the rudest couple I have ever delt with. They double booked our spot and before I setup our tent I went back to the office twice and spoke with the husband of the manager to double check the spot he was telling me was correct, he just shrugged me off both times saying Yes your between those two vans... I had about 2mtrs to setup our tent and turned out I was actually between 2 spots because one of them wasn't ment to be there. Hours after I had setup our tent the manager came passed and informed me I wasn't in a correct spot and the van next to me was actually in my spot, she said she would refund me one night but I'd have to pack up everything and move, the next day I went into the office and spoke with the husband again, I said we'd be packing up and leaving as I wasn't moving the tent twice, he refunded me $60 out of the $138 it cost me, he said I'd have to speak with his wife, I called back after we left and copped a spray from the wife about not asking before I setup the tent, I told her I spoke to her husband twice and he didn't bother checking so it was hardly my fault. 1st and last time we'll be back and we're locals.
Beautiful place!!!
Loved this place. So friendly and close to the beach, very well set up for families with young kids with good baby facilities.
